sources.label: StockTitan - HSAI latest news feed (no new items since Jun 6; AGM Jun 26 / 8-for-1 split announced May 26)
Yahoo Finance - HSAI down post Q1 despite Mercedes L3 lidar win and return to profitability (pre-Jun 6 context)
Globe and Mail - Hesai Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (shipments +140.9% YoY, pre-Jun 6 context)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already re-rated HSAI up (14 to 11) on the sensing-purity and commercialization case; nothing genuinely new has hit since June 6, so no second move. Today's -4.3% drift on zero news deepens the -23.9% YTD drawdown with the robotics thesis intact, which marginally improves asymmetry at rank 11 - worth +1 score, not another rank jump.
verdicts.bull: Lidar shipments grew 140.9% YoY in Q1 with a return to profitability, Mercedes-Benz L3 design win, and #1 share across humanoid/quadruped/robotaxi lidar submarkets - concrete robotics revenue, not narrative, at a $3.25B cap down 24% YTD.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 guidance had already missed consensus and the stock has been heavy since; no new datapoints since June 6, so the setup is unchanged from yesterday's re-rating, and the June 26 AGM (8-for-1 split) is housekeeping, not fundamentals.
verdicts.bear: ASP compression in China lidar and reliance on Chinese ADAS volume remain unresolved, and as a US-listed China ADR the name carries persistent geopolitical/delisting flow risk that can keep crushing multiples regardless of unit growth.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results / robotics-segment shipment disclosure plus any new humanoid or robotaxi design-win announcements; AGM June 26 (8-for-1 split) as a minor liquidity event.
verdicts.primaryRisk: China ADR risk-off flows and lidar ASP deflation eroding the margin recovery despite record unit volumes.
